title_short: Paradox of Post-Pandemic Travel
method: Gamma GLM with log-link for journey time + longitudinal traffic count analysis
key_finding: Post-pandemic car travel fell 11.6% in Cambridge but journey times rose 12% due to road space reallocation; precipitation increases car use while reducing cycling
innovation: Uses Gamma GLM (same model family as our approach) with comprehensive controls (hour/day/month/school term/weather/events/roadworks); DARe-funded Cambridge study

title_short: Variability of Transport Emissions
method: Latent Profile Models + NTS diary data analysis
key_finding: Intra-week variability in transport emissions is substantial; speed-emissions relationship documented (Figure A1); aggregate SEC-emissions correlation dissolves when compositional factors controlled
innovation: Latent profile approach identifies unobserved traveller groups; methodological caution for rainfall-speed studies about compositional confounding

title_short: Empirical Studies on Traffic Flow in Inclement Weather
method: Empirical analysis of loop detector data across 3 US cities
key_finding: Rain reduces free-flow speed by 2-6.5% and capacity by 4-11%; snow has larger effects
innovation: Developed weather adjustment factors for free-flow speed and speed-at-capacity and capacity and jam density

title_short: Resilience Analysis Road Traffic to Rainfall
method: Probabilistic modelling + hierarchical clustering
key_finding: System resilience decreases 6.7% per 10mm rainfall increase below 50mm; 66% low-resilience segments near commercial areas
innovation: Links road environment characteristics (land use and segment length) to resilience patterns

title_short: Assessing Transport Network Resilience: Empirical Review
method: Systematic literature review (53 papers from 127)
key_finding: Spatial patterns of resilience are influenced by network topology and land use; empirical evidence still fragmented
innovation: First systematic review focusing exclusively on empirical (real-world data) resilience studies

authors: Ganin, Kitsak, Marchese, Keisler, Seager, Linkov
title_short: Resilience and Efficiency in Transportation Networks
method: Network topology analysis + percolation theory
key_finding: Efficient urban road networks are often fragile; resilient networks may be inefficient -- revealing a resilience-efficiency paradox
innovation: First large-scale demonstration that resilience and efficiency are distinct and sometimes competing properties of transport networks

title_short: Methodology for Road Traffic Resilience Analysis
method: Link Performance Index for Resilience (LPIR)
key_finding: Road section resilience depends on both resistance to and recovery from traffic disturbances; everyday events matter not just disasters
innovation: Proposes LPIR indicator covering both resistance and recovery phases for individual road sections

title_short: Impact of Flooding on Road Transport: Depth-Disruption
method: Curve fitting to video analysis + existing data (depth-disruption function)
key_finding: Flood water depth has a nonlinear relationship with vehicle speed reduction (R-squared=0.95); binary open/closed assumption is inadequate
innovation: Introduces continuous depth-disruption function replacing binary flood impact assumption

authors: Farahmand, Yin, Hsu, Savadogo, Espinet Alegre, Mostafavi
title_short: Integrating Climate Projections and Probabilistic Network Analysis
method: Climate downscaling + probabilistic network failure analysis
key_finding: Climate projections can be integrated with network vulnerability models to identify future critical links under changing precipitation patterns
innovation: Bridges climate science projections with transport network resilience planning at regional scale

title_short: Flood-Induced Traffic Congestion: Agent-Based Simulation Bristol
method: Agent-based simulation (MATSim) + risk-based speed reduction
key_finding: Flood-induced congestion propagates far beyond directly flooded areas; accessibility loss is spatially uneven across Bristol
innovation: Couples flood hazard maps with agent-based dynamic traffic assignment to capture congestion cascade effects

authors: Huang, Cui, Zhang, Tong, Shi, Liu
title_short: Overview of Agent-Based Models for Transport Simulation
method: Systematic review of ABM tools across micro/meso/macro scales
key_finding: ABMs excel at emergent behaviour but require significant computational resources; weather can be modelled as autonomous 'disruptor agent'; MATSim/SUMO/TRANSIMS dominate; BDI and PECS frameworks underused
innovation: Comprehensive taxonomy of ABM toolkits with time-scale classification; identifies weather-as-disruptor-agent pattern; supports empirical approaches as faster alternative for operational analysis

title_short: Stress Test Framework for Transport System Resilience
method: Stress testing framework (qualitative + quantitative)
key_finding: Standardized stress testing can identify critical transport system vulnerabilities to climate hazards and guide adaptation investment
innovation: First international standardized framework for transport system climate resilience stress testing

title_short: Climate Change and Transport Infrastructure: REA
method: Rapid Evidence Assessment (34 papers from 158 screened)
key_finding: Adaptation benefits outweigh costs; precipitation reduces visibility and speed (not just closures); 2012 Newcastle flood: 377 road links flooded from 50mm in <2hrs; macro costs = 2.2x direct repair costs; QUADRO model for pricing delay
innovation: Comprehensive UK evidence synthesis explicitly identifying speed degradation (not just closure) as recognised impact pathway; cost-benefit framework for adaptation investment

title_short: Transport Infrastructure Resilience: Bibliometric Analysis
method: Bibliometric + keyword co-occurrence network + thematic analysis of 2000+ publications
key_finding: Four clusters identified; rainfall-transport sits at intersection of Cluster 1 (climate impacts) and Cluster 2 (risk assessment); calls for integrated simulation platforms combining urban climatology and network science
innovation: First comprehensive bibliometric mapping of 30 years (1995-2025); validates rainfall-transport as active research frontier; identifies gap in real-time weather-to-performance linkage

title_short: Resilience of Transport Networks to Road Failures
method: Gravity-model-based traffic centrality + OSM/GHSL data + German Mobility Panel
key_finding: Ahr Valley 2021 flood: bridge destruction caused cascading delays far from epicenter; German Mobility Panel shows car usage increases with rainfall intensity (demand-side effect)
innovation: Novel gravity-model traffic centrality with Delta-T commuter time metric; open-data approach (OSM+GHSL); binary failure model -- gap for continuous speed degradation

title_short: Transport Networks and Climate Change: Centrality Measures Review
method: Review of 17 centrality measures across 4 categories (degree/closeness/betweenness/eigenvector)
key_finding: Betweenness and closeness centrality should use transit time not distance; information centrality most relevant for disruption assessment; current models assume ideal weather -- gap for rainfall-speed integration
innovation: Systematic assessment linking centrality measures to climate adaptation; explicitly identifies weather-gap in traffic flow models

title_short: Impact of Rainfall on Driving Speed: Radar + FCD
method: Linear regression on radar rainfall + floating car data
key_finding: Heavy rainfall (>8L/m2 in 5min) reduces highway speeds by >30% at 130km/h limit zones; effect scales with speed limit
innovation: Combines high-resolution weather radar with 1.5M road-section floating car data across Germany

title_short: Rainfall Intensity Effects on Heteroscedastic Speed Dispersion
method: Exponential regression on video detector + rain gauge data
key_finding: Speed dispersion (CV 0.05-0.2) increases with rainfall intensity; exponential function fits speed-rainfall relationship well
innovation: Models heteroscedastic (varying variance) speed dispersion under rainfall rather than just mean speed

title_short: Data-Driven Analysis of Weather Impacts on Urban Traffic
method: Regression + Decision Trees on city-level traffic data
key_finding: Precipitation causes congestion and delays especially during peak hours; effects vary by city characteristics
innovation: City-level data-driven framework linking weather variations to traffic conditions using machine learning

authors: Li, Wu, Xiao, Hu, Wang, Chen
title_short: Highway Network Resilience Under Rainfall: Percolation Theory
method: Data-driven percolation theory
key_finding: Speed decline rate serves as effective percolation indicator; critical rainfall thresholds identified for network fragmentation (East Midlands UK case)
innovation: Applies percolation theory to rainfall-induced functional degradation rather than complete link removal

title_short: Road Transport Resilience Under Extreme Rainfall
method: Dynamic traffic assignment (link transmission model)
key_finding: Integrating flood depth and visibility reduction and signal failure reveals much larger network delays than considering any single factor alone
innovation: First framework integrating three rainfall impact pathways (flood/visibility/signals) with delay propagation

title_short: Impact of Urban Road Characteristics on Vehicle Speed
method: MLR on V85 with forward selection (48K+ spot speeds; 240 locations; 130 segments in Brescia Italy)
key_finding: R²_adj=85.8% for final model (9 predictors); MAE=2.77 km/h; segment length/lanes/trees/asphalt/markings increase V85; parking/obstacles/intersection density decrease V85; traffic calming measures NOT significant
innovation: Largest urban V85 sample; introduces novel predictors (left crossbar width/obstacles); demonstrates road geometry dominates over traffic calming; assumes normality (contrast with Gamma GLM approach)

title_short: Only 7% of UK SRN Meets Climate Standards
method: Investigative journalism / data reporting
key_finding: Only 7% of UK's 7400km strategic road network meets climate drainage standards set 20 years ago
innovation: Reveals critical UK infrastructure adaptation deficit with specific quantitative evidence

title_short: Climate Change and the Strategic Road Network
method: Climate risk assessment + adaptation planning
key_finding: 38% of English roads at flood risk in 2024 rising to 46% by 2050; UK may see 0.9-5.4C warming by 2070s
innovation: Official UK strategic road network climate adaptation reporting under ARP4

title_short: Modelling AADT on Local Roads
method: OLS and GWR regression on land use + road + Census data (North Carolina)
key_finding: GWR significantly outperforms OLS (spatial non-stationarity key); road density/nearest nonlocal AADT/land use predict local road volumes; high prediction errors in urban counties and dead-end roads
innovation: Demonstrates GWR superiority for spatially-varying AADT; supports spatially-varying coefficients in any road performance model; baseline traffic estimation methodology for minor roads

title_short: Generalized Linear Models (2nd ed.)
method: Theoretical framework for GLMs
key_finding: GLMs unify regression and ANOVA under exponential family distributions with link functions
innovation: Foundational statistical reference establishing GLM theory and practice

title_short: Generalized Linear Models
method: Iterative weighted linear regression for exponential family
key_finding: Maximum likelihood estimation via iterative weighted least squares for exponential family distributions
innovation: Seminal paper introducing the GLM framework that unified diverse statistical models

title_short: Measures of Centrality Based on Betweenness
method: Graph-theoretic betweenness measures
key_finding: Betweenness centrality captures a node's control potential over communication in networks
innovation: Introduced betweenness centrality as a formal measure of node importance in networks
